displayNotes: Use cards for scanability on iPhone: one primary number, status pill, and 2–3 details.
Use baseline/range visualization for HRV and RHR because personal baseline matters more than population comparison.
Use progress bars for readiness, sleep score, Body Battery, stress, steps, intensity minutes, training load, and nutrition.
This 5 AM run labels Garmin sync windows explicitly and marks partial-day activity plus unpopulated readiness/ACWR rather than guessing.

Source fields include training readiness when available, HRV baseline, sleep score/duration/stages/need, Body Battery wake/current/high/charge/drain, stress average/max/rest split, steps/distance/active calories, weekly intensity minutes, activity load when acute load/ACWR are unavailable, VO₂ max, nutrition calories/protein/water, resting HR vs 7-day average, SpO₂, and respiration.
